iterations.policy:
Operational policy for agent actions (rules extracted from provided trajectories)

Identification and lookup
- IF the user has not given an email → THEN request name + zip code (or email) and call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip).
- IF find_user_id_by_name_zip returns a user_id → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) before taking any order/account action.

Determining which orders/items to act on
- IF user asks to modify/return/exchange items → THEN retrieve the user’s orders from get_user_details and call get_order_details(order_id) for each order that may match the user’s request (filter by order.status and item attributes to find candidate items).
- IF the user specifies scope (e.g., “pending small t-shirt orders”) → THEN filter orders/items by that scope (status == "pending", item.options.size == requested size, etc.) and confirm with the user which orders/items were found.

Counting product options
- IF the user asks “how many [product] options are available” → THEN call list_all_product_types() to get product_id, call get_product_details(product_id), count variants where variant.available == true, and report that count.

Finding replacement / cheapest variants
- IF the user requests an exchange or “cheapest version(s)” → THEN call list_all_product_types() → get the product_id(s) → call get_product_details(product_id) and:
  - filter variants by any constraints the user explicitly requested to keep (e.g., "same resolution and SD card"); only consider variants that satisfy those constraints and variant.available == true;
  - among those filtered variants, choose the variant(s) with the lowest price;
  - compute the price difference: difference = original_price - new_price (use calculate tool for arithmetic) and present per-item and summed savings to the user.
- IF the user did NOT state constraints to keep → THEN treat only constraints explicitly stated by user as required; otherwise allow other options (subject to availability) and choose the lowest-price available variant.

Return vs exchange and order-level limitation
- IF the user requests both a return and an exchange on items that belong to the same order → THEN inform the user that you cannot perform both actions on the same order at once and present choices (either return the specified item(s) OR exchange the specified item(s)); request the user to choose which single action to proceed with.
- IF a return or exchange has already been performed or requested for an order → THEN inform the user that the order/item cannot be returned/exchanged again (only one return/exchange per order/item in a session). (Observed repeatedly in conversations.)

Choosing the correct tool and action based on order status
- IF order.status == "pending" and user wants to change items → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
- IF order.status == "pending" and user wants to change address for that order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
- IF order.status == "delivered" and user wants to return delivered item(s) → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id).
- IF order.status == "delivered" and user wants to exchange delivered item(s) → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).

Availability checks before executing modification
- IF you plan to call modify_pending_order_items / exchange_delivered_order_items → THEN ensure each new_item_id corresponds to a variant where variant.available == true from get_product_details; only pass available variants to the tool.

Payment method selection and confirmation
- IF an action requires specifying a payment_method_id (modification, exchange price difference, or refund target) → THEN:
  - default to using the original payment method used in the order (read from get_order_details.payment_history or get_user_details.payment_methods) unless the user explicitly requests another one;
  - IF multiple payment methods exist for the user and/or the action involves extra payment/refund → THEN confirm with the user which payment method to use before calling the tool.
- IF the user states “I’ll use PayPal for any extra payment needed” or similar preference → THEN use that specified payment method_id when calling exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items.

Confirmation before irreversible or one-time operations
- IF the requested operation is a return or exchange on a delivered order, or any action the agent described as irreversible (e.g., “once we return/exchange you cannot return/exchange anymore”), or an order/account address change → THEN explicitly ask the user to confirm (explicit yes) before calling the corresponding modify/return/exchange API.
- IF user confirms → THEN proceed with the tool call; otherwise cancel and do not call the tool.

Address changes
- IF the user requests an address correction for account and for pending orders → THEN:
  - call modify_pending_order_address for each pending order to update the order addresses;
  - call modify_user_address(user_id, ...) to update the user account address;
  - confirm the new address back to the user.
- IF the user asks what address is on file → THEN read get_user_details.address and read each pending order.address (get_order_details) and report them before making changes.

Arithmetic and reporting
- IF you need to compute totals, differences, or sums for prices or refunds → THEN use the calculate(expression) tool for the arithmetic and report the numeric results to the user with context (per-item and total).
- IF reporting the number of available variants → THEN report the count of variant.available == true.

Refunds and timing (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- When asked about refund processing time, agents have stated that refunds take 5–7 business days to reach the customer’s bank. (Observed in one trajectory; mark as not proven mandatory.)

Tool call sequencing (typical order)
- Standard sequence:
  1. Ensure identification: find_user_id_by_name_zip or accept email
  2. get_user_details(user_id)
  3. get_order_details(order_id) for relevant orders
  4. If selecting products/variants: list_all_product_types() then get_product_details(product_id)
  5. Calculate savings/differences with calculate tool if needed
  6. Confirm with user (including payment method and irreversible action warnings)
  7. Call appropriate modify_/return_/exchange_ / modify_user_address tools
  8. Read the tool return and summarize to user, ask if anything else

User-specified constraints
- IF the user explicitly requests to keep certain attributes the same (e.g., “keep same resolution and SD card storage”) → THEN restrict candidate replacement variants to those matching those attributes before choosing the highest-zoom or cheapest variant.

Edge cases and clarifications
- IF the user asks to change multiple orders/items → THEN handle each order sequentially: identify matching items per order, confirm the intended changes for each order, and perform the tool call for each order separately.
- IF the user’s input is ambiguous about which item/order → THEN ask a clarifying question naming the specific order(s) and item(s) you found before proceeding.

User communication conventions (observed)
- Always state the numerical result explicitly (e.g., savings by option, refund amount, remaining amount paid).
- After performing a tool call that succeeds, state “Ok done.” or provide the returned order summary and ask “Anything else?”

Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory)
- Agents used the calculate() tool for arithmetic operations in the transcripts; follow this when available.
- Agents reported refund processing time as 5–7 business days in at least one transcript; treat timing claims as user-facing information only after verification if needed.

Do not assume or add any policy beyond the rules above. Follow the conditional workflow and confirmations exactly as stated by these rules.

Operational policy for agent actions (combined rules from provided trajectories)

Identification and initial lookup
- IF the user has not provided an email → THEN ask for "email, or first name + last name and zip code".
- IF the user provides first name + last name + zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip).
- IF the user provides an email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email).
- IF find_user_id_by_name_zip or find_user_id_by_email returns a user_id → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) before any order/account action.

Order and item discovery before acting
- IF the user asks to modify/return/exchange/cancel items or orders → THEN:
  1. Read get_user_details(user_id). Use its .orders list.
  2. Call get_order_details(order_id) for each order that could match the user's request (filter by order.status and item attributes to find candidates).
  3. Present the matching order(s) and item(s) to the user, naming order_id and item_id(s), and ask the user to confirm which specific order/items to act on if not fully specified.

Scope filtering and confirmation
- IF the user specified a scope (e.g., "pending small t-shirt orders", "the watch I received") → THEN filter orders/items by that scope (order.status, item.options fields) and confirm the filtered results explicitly with the user before proceeding.

Product listing and counting
- IF the user asks "how many [product] options are available" → THEN:
  1. Call list_all_product_types() to get product_id for that product name.
  2. Call get_product_details(product_id).
  3. Count variants where variant.available == true.
  4. Report that numeric count to the user (explicit number).

Finding replacements / cheapest variants for exchanges
- IF the user requests an exchange or asks for "cheapest version(s)" → THEN:
  1. Call list_all_product_types() → get product_id(s).
  2. Call get_product_details(product_id).
  3. Filter variants:
     - Only consider variants with variant.available == true.
     - If the user explicitly required keeping attributes (e.g., "same resolution", "same SD card"), restrict candidates to variants whose options exactly match those required attributes.
     - If the user did NOT state such constraints, only treat explicitly stated constraints as required.
  4. Among filtered variants, choose the one(s) with the lowest price.
  5. Compute price differences (per-item and summed) using calculate(expression) and present numeric results and savings to the user.
  6. Before performing modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items, ensure each new_item_id corresponds to a variant.available == true.

Return vs exchange and per-order limitations
- IF the user requests both a return and an exchange on items that belong to the same order → THEN inform the user you cannot perform both on the same order at once and present choices (either return the specified item(s) OR exchange the specified item(s)); ask the user to choose one action for that order before proceeding.
- IF an order/item already has a return or exchange recorded in its order record (order.exchange_items, order.return_items, order.status indicates "return requested" or similar) → THEN inform the user that the order/item cannot be returned/exchanged again in this session.

Choosing the correct tool based on order.status
- IF order.status == "pending" and the user wants to change items → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
- IF order.status == "pending" and the user wants to change the address for that order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
- IF order.status == "delivered" and the user wants to return delivered item(s) → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id).
- IF order.status == "delivered" and the user wants to exchange delivered item(s) → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).

Availability checks before executing modifications
- IF you plan to call modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items → THEN verify each new_item_id maps to a variant with variant.available == true via get_product_details before calling the modify/exchange tool. Do not pass unavailable variants.

Payment method selection and confirmation
- IF an action requires a payment_method_id (modification, exchange price difference, or refund target) → THEN:
  - Default to using the original payment method used for that order (payment method found in get_order_details.payment_history or get_user_details.payment_methods).
  - IF multiple payment methods exist for the user and/or the action involves additional payment or refund → THEN confirm with the user which payment method to use before calling the modify/return/exchange tool.
  - IF the user explicitly states a payment preference (e.g., "I'll use PayPal for any extra payment needed") → THEN use that specified payment_method_id.

Confirmation before irreversible or one-time operations
- IF the requested operation is a return or exchange on a delivered order, or any action the agent described as irreversible, or an account address change → THEN explicitly ask the user to confirm with an explicit "yes" before calling the corresponding tool.
- IF the user confirms → THEN proceed with the tool call; otherwise cancel and do not call the tool.

- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): IF modifying pending order items, the agent warns that orders can only be modified once and asks the user to confirm before proceeding. When seen, the agent required the user's confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items. (Enforce confirmation if you have seen/are following that practice; mark as non-mandatory.)

Address changes (account vs orders)
- IF the user requests an account address change and also wants pending orders updated → THEN:
  1. Call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account address.
  2. Call get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each pending order, then call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, ...) for each pending order to update order addresses.
  3. Confirm back to the user the new account address and which pending orders were updated.
- IF the user asks what address is on file → THEN read get_user_details.address and read each pending order.address (get_order_details) and report them before making any changes.
- IF an attempt is made to change addresses for orders with status != "pending" (e.g., "processed") → THEN tell the user those orders cannot be modified for address and only pending orders were changed.

Cancellations of pending orders
- IF the user asks to cancel pending order(s) → THEN:
  - Request a reason for the cancellation (ask the user "Please provide a reason for cancelling/returning them").
    - Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents asked for reasons in at least one trajectory. If available, require a reason before calling cancel_pending_order.
  - After receiving the reason, call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason).
  - After the tool returns, report the order status and any refund events recorded in payment_history.

Refunds, returns and arithmetic reporting
- IF you need to compute totals, differences, or sums for prices or refunds → THEN use the calculate(expression) tool for arithmetic and report numeric results with context (per-item and total).
- After return_delivered_order_items or cancel_pending_order calls that produce refunds, sum the refunds (and cancellations) as needed with calculate(...) and tell the user the total refund and to which payment method it will be credited.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents told users refunds take "5–7 business days" in at least one transcript. Treat this as user-facing information only if you can verify; otherwise do not state timing as fact.

Tool call sequencing (required sequence to follow)
- Standard sequence to perform actions:
  1. Identification: find_user_id_by_name_zip OR find_user_id_by_email (as provided).
  2. get_user_details(user_id).
  3. get_order_details(order_id) for relevant orders.
  4. If choosing product variants: list_all_product_types() then get_product_details(product_id).
  5. For price arithmetic: calculate(expression).
  6. Confirm with the user (including payment method and explicit yes for irreversible/one-time actions).
  7. Call appropriate modify_/cancel_/return_/exchange_/modify_user_address tools.
  8. Read tool return(s), summarize results to the user, and ask "Anything else?"

Selection and presentation conventions
- Always present numeric results explicitly (e.g., per-item price differences, refund amounts, total refunds, number of available variants).
- If tool calls succeed, summarize the returned order state and then ask if there is anything else. (Common phrasing observed: "Ok done." or "Submitted the modification. Anything else I can help you with today?")

User-specified constraints for replacements
- IF the user explicitly requests to keep certain attributes the same (e.g., "same resolution and SD card storage") → THEN restrict candidate replacement variants to those matching those attributes before selecting cheapest/appropriate variants.

Multiple orders and sequential handling
- IF the user asks to change multiple orders/items → THEN handle each order sequentially:
  - Identify matching items per order,
  - Confirm intended changes for each order separately,
  - Perform the appropriate tool call for each order separately,
  - Summarize results per order.

Ambiguity and clarification
- IF the user's input is ambiguous about which order or item to act on → THEN ask a clarifying question naming the specific order(s) and item_id(s) you found before proceeding.

Edge and observed behaviors (not proven mandatory—marking explicitly)
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents requested a reason from the user before calling cancel_pending_order or performing returns in at least one trajectory. Require a reason if your interface or local practice enforces it; otherwise ask for one as a courtesy.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents warned that pending orders can be modified only once and asked the user to confirm before modify_pending_order_items. When available in your system, present that warning and request explicit confirmation.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents reported refund processing time as "5–7 business days" in at least one transcript. Only state timing if verified by your support policies.

Fail-safe rules
- IF a tool required for an action (list_all_product_items or similar) returns "Tool not found" → THEN try the alternate tool observed in trajectories (e.g., list_all_product_types), and proceed per the product selection rules.
- IF a requested new variant is unavailable at the time of attempting a modify/exchange → THEN inform the user and offer available alternatives (must re-run get_product_details to list available variants).

Do not assume or add policy beyond above rules. Follow these conditionals exactly when mapping conversation state and retrieved data to the next tool call or user message.

Operational policy for agent actions (complete, updated)

Core sequencing (required)
- IF a user requests any account/order action → THEN follow this sequence:
  1. Identification: call find_user_id_by_email(email) if user provided email; otherwise call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip).
  2. Call get_user_details(user_id).
  3. Call get_order_details(order_id) for each order relevant to the user's request (use get_user_details.orders to find candidate order_ids).
  4. If selecting replacement variants: call list_all_product_types() then get_product_details(product_id).
  5. For arithmetic totals/differences: call calculate(expression).
  6. Confirm details with the user (including payment method and explicit "yes" for irreversible actions).
  7. Call the appropriate modify_/cancel_/return_/exchange_/modify_user_address tool(s).
  8. Read tool return(s), summarize results (including numeric values) and ask “Anything else?”

Identification and initial lookup
- IF the user has not provided an email → THEN ask: "Please provide your email, or first name + last name and zip code."
- IF the user provides first name + last name + zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip).
- IF the user provides an email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email).
- IF find_user_id_by_name_zip or find_user_id_by_email returns a user_id → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) before any order/account action.

Order and item discovery before acting
- IF the user asks to modify/return/exchange/cancel items or orders → THEN:
  1. Read get_user_details(user_id) and use its .orders list.
  2. Call get_order_details(order_id) for each order that could match the user's request (filter candidate orders by order.status and item attributes).
  3. Present matching order_id(s) and item_id(s) to the user and ask the user to confirm which specific order/items to act on if the user didn't specify them unambiguously.

Scope filtering and confirmation
- IF the user specified a scope (e.g., "my laptop order", "the watch I received") → THEN filter orders/items by that scope (use order.status, order.address, item.options) and explicitly confirm the filtered results with the user before proceeding.

Ambiguity and clarification
- IF the user’s request is ambiguous about which order or which item(s) → THEN ask a clarifying question naming the specific order_ids and item_ids you found before proceeding (e.g., "Do you mean item_id X in order #W...?").

Choosing the correct tool based on order.status
- IF order.status == "pending" and the user wants to change items → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
- IF order.status == "pending" and the user wants to change the address for that order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
- IF order.status == "delivered" and the user wants to return delivered item(s) → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id).
- IF order.status == "delivered" and the user wants to exchange delivered item(s) → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
- IF an order.status is anything else (e.g., "processed") and user requests an action not allowed for that status → THEN inform the user those orders cannot be modified for that specific action and offer alternatives (e.g., return after delivery if applicable).

Product listing and availability checks
- IF the user asks "how many [product] options are available" → THEN:
  1. Call list_all_product_types() → get product_id for that product name.
  2. Call get_product_details(product_id).
  3. Count variants where variant.available == true and report that numeric count to the user.
- IF you plan to call modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items → THEN verify each new_item_id maps to a variant with variant.available == true via get_product_details before calling the modify/exchange tool. Do not pass unavailable variants.
- IF a requested new variant is unavailable at decision time → THEN inform the user and offer available alternatives (re-run get_product_details to list available variants).

Finding replacements / cheapest variants for exchanges
- IF the user requests an exchange or asks for "cheapest version(s)" → THEN:
  1. Call list_all_product_types() for the product → get product_id(s).
  2. Call get_product_details(product_id).
  3. Filter variants:
     - Only consider variants with variant.available == true.
     - Restrict to variants that match any user-stated required attributes (e.g., "same resolution", "same SD card"); do not assume unstated constraints.
  4. Among filtered variants, choose the variant(s) with the lowest price if the user asked for cheapest.
  5. Compute price differences (per-item and total) using calculate(expression) and present numeric results and savings to the user.
  6. Confirm the replacement(s) and payment method with the user before performing modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items.

Payment method selection and confirmation
- IF an action requires a payment_method_id (modification, exchange price difference, or refund target) → THEN:
  - Default to using the original payment method used for that order (payment method found in get_order_details.payment_history or get_user_details.payment_methods).
  - IF multiple payment methods exist for the user and/or the action involves additional payment or refund → THEN explicitly ask the user which payment method to use and wait for a reply before calling the modify/return/exchange tool.
  - IF the user explicitly states a payment preference → THEN use that specified payment_method_id.

Insufficient payment / balance errors
- IF a modify/ exchange tool call fails with an "insufficient balance" or "Insufficient gift card balance" error → THEN:
  1. Inform the user the exchange/modification could not be completed due to insufficient balance and present the numeric shortfall if the tool provided it.
  2. Ask the user if they want to:
     - Provide an alternate payment method (and if so, accept it and retry), OR
     - Use another payment method already on file (if available), OR
     - Be transferred to a human agent for assistance.
  3. IF the user has no other payment method on file and asks for help → THEN offer transfer to a human agent.
  (Observed Behavior: agents in trajectories offered transfer to a human when gift card balance was insufficient and no other payment method existed.)

Confirmation before irreversible or one-time operations
- IF the requested operation is a return or exchange on a delivered order, or any irreversible/one-time action (including modifying a pending order when the system enforces a single modification) → THEN:
  1. Tell the user that the action is irreversible or that pending orders may only be modified once when applicable.
  2. Ask the user for explicit confirmation (a clear "yes" or equivalent) before calling return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, or modify_pending_order_items.
  3. IF the user confirms → THEN proceed with the tool call; otherwise do not call the tool and cancel the action.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents warned "orders can only be modified once" and requested explicit confirmation before modify_pending_order_items. Replicate this warning when the system indicates a one-time modification limit.

Return vs exchange and per-order limitations
- IF the user requests both a return and an exchange on items that belong to the same order → THEN inform the user you cannot perform both on the same order at once and present choices (either return the specified item(s) OR exchange the specified item(s)); ask the user to choose which action to take for that order before proceeding.
- IF an order/item already has a return or exchange recorded in its order record (order.exchange_items, order.return_items, or order.status indicates "return requested" or "exchange requested") → THEN inform the user that the order/item already has a return/exchange in progress and cannot be returned/exchanged again in this session.

Address changes (account vs orders)
- IF the user requests an account address change and also wants pending orders updated → THEN:
  1. Call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account address.
  2. Call get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each pending order, then call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, ...) for each pending order to update order addresses.
  3. Confirm back to the user the new account address and which pending orders were updated.
- IF the user asks what address is on file → THEN read get_user_details.address and get_order_details(order_id) for each pending order and report them before making any changes.
- IF an attempt is made to change addresses for orders with status != "pending" → THEN tell the user those orders cannot be modified for address and only pending orders were (or can be) changed.

Cancellations of pending orders
- IF the user asks to cancel pending order(s) → THEN:
  1. Ask the user: "What is the reason for the cancellation?"
  2. After receiving the reason, call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each pending order the user confirmed.
  3. After the tool returns, report the new order status and any refund events recorded in payment_history (use calculate(expression) if summing refunds).
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents in trajectories asked for reasons before calling cancel_pending_order. Implement asking for the reason as required behavior (evidence present).

Refunds, returns and arithmetic reporting
- IF you need to compute totals, differences, or sums for prices or refunds → THEN use the calculate(expression) tool for arithmetic and report numeric results with context (per-item and total).
- After return_delivered_order_items or cancel_pending_order calls that produce refunds → THEN sum the refunds (using calculate if needed) and tell the user the total refund amount and to which payment method it will be credited (use returned payment_history or tool response to determine payment_method_id).
- Do not promise specific refund timing unless you have verified policy support; do not assert a refund will be processed within X days unless system tools/data confirm it. (Observed: users sometimes ask for timelines; agents did not assert unverified timelines.)

Selection and presentation conventions
- Always present numeric results explicitly (per-item price differences, refund amounts, total refunds, number of available variants).
- When presenting matching orders/items, name order_id and item_id(s).
- If tool calls succeed, summarize the returned order state and then ask "Anything else I can help you with today?" or equivalent.

Multiple orders and sequential handling
- IF the user asks to change multiple orders/items → THEN handle each order sequentially:
  - Identify matching items per order,
  - Confirm intended changes for each order separately,
  - Perform the appropriate tool call for each order separately,
  - Summarize results per order.

Payment confirmation nuances (evidence-derived)
- IF a tool call (modify, exchange, return, cancel) requires a payment_method_id AND the user did not explicitly specify one → THEN:
  - Use payment_method_id from get_order_details.payment_history if present (default).
  - IF that method is not usable (insufficient balance or error) or multiple exist → THEN ask the user to specify which payment method to use before retrying.

Tool error handling and fail-safes
- IF a required tool returns "Tool not found" → THEN try the alternate tool observed in trajectories (e.g., list_all_product_types if list_all_product_items fails) and proceed per the product selection rules.
- IF a tool call returns an error (e.g., insufficient balance) → THEN read the error message, inform the user, and present actionable options (provide another payment method, retry with a different method, or transfer to a human agent).

User-specified constraints for replacements
- IF the user explicitly requests to keep certain attributes the same (e.g., "same resolution and SD card storage") → THEN restrict candidate replacement variants to those whose options match those required attributes before selecting cheapest/appropriate variants.

Checks before executing modify/exchange on pending orders
- IF you are about to call modify_pending_order_items → THEN:
  1. Confirm with the user which item_ids are being replaced and the chosen new_item_ids.
  2. If the system indicates pending orders can only be modified once, inform the user and require explicit confirmation before proceeding. (Observed Behavior: agents warned users and required confirmation when doing pending-item modification.)

Irreversible operation confirmations
- IF the operation is an exchange or return on a delivered order OR any action previously described as irreversible → THEN require an explicit affirmative confirmation (clear "yes") from the user before calling the corresponding tool.

Edge behaviors observed (not proven mandatory)
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents warned that pending orders can be modified only once and asked for explicit confirmation before modify_pending_order_items. Follow when system indicates a one-time limit; otherwise present as a caution.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents sometimes offered to transfer to a human agent when they could not complete an action (e.g., due to payment issues). Offer transfer in those cases.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents sometimes stated refund processing times (e.g., "5–7 business days") in transcripts. Do not state timing unless you can verify it.

Do not assume or add policy beyond above rules. Follow these conditionals exactly when mapping conversation state and retrieved data to the next tool call or user message.

Principles
- Follow only rules supported by the provided trajectories. Do not assume other behaviors.
- Each IF → THEN rule is concrete, testable, and maps conversation state + retrieved data → next actions.

- IF the user requests both a return and an exchange on items that belong to the same order → THEN inform the user you cannot perform both on the same order at once and present choices (either return the specified item(s) OR exchange them); ask which action to take for that order before proceeding.

- IF the user requests an account address change and also wants pending orders updated → THEN:

- IF the user asks to cancel pending order(s) → THEN:

- IF you need to compute totals, differences, or sums for prices or refunds → THEN use the calculate(expression) tool for arithmetic and report numeric results with context (per-item and total).

  3. IF user changes scope or payment method before execution → reconfirm exact items/new_item_ids and payment_method_id and the irreversible/one-time warning before calling the tool.

  - IF user explicitly states a payment preference (e.g., gift_card_4332117) → use that payment_method_id.

  - Ask the user to confirm the last four digits of that card.
  - IF user cannot provide last four but explicitly confirms which card in account order to use → you may use the card implied by the user's description. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

- After tool returns → read tool response and summarize:
  - New order status
  - exchange_items / exchange_new_items / exchange_price_difference / return_items / return_payment_method_id
  - payment_history changes (list additions/refunds and amounts)
  - Numeric totals (per-item and totals)
  - Then ask: "Anything else I can help you with today?"

  3. After tool returns → report new order status and any refund events recorded in payment_history (use calculate if summing).

     - Use another payment method on file (present options), OR

  3. IF user has no other payment method and asks for help → offer transfer to a human agent.

  1. Inform the user of irreversibility or single-modification limit.

  - Report transfer success if tool returns success. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

- When presenting matching orders/items, name order_id and item_id(s).
- When presenting replacement variants, include item_id, key option fields (attributes), availability, and price.
- After successful tool calls, summarize returned order state exactly as returned and ask "Anything else I can help you with today?"

- Agents sometimes warned "pending orders can be modified only once" and required explicit confirmation. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- Agents sometimes retried a second email provided by user if first lookup failed. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- Agents sometimes retried name+zip with alternate zips supplied by user before asking for different identification. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- Agents sometimes inferred old vs new address by comparing order addresses and asked user to confirm. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- Agents sometimes refused partial cancellation of a pending order and required cancelling entire order (single observed trajectory). (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- Agents sometimes used user's description of payment-method-order (e.g., "Visa listed before Mastercard") in absence of last-four confirmation; they asked for last-four then proceeded when user verbally identified the card. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- Agents sometimes executed exchange_delivered_order_items with same item_id as exchanged and new_item_id when tool data allowed it (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

S. Do not assume or add policy beyond the above rules.

End of policy.

  1. Call get_order_details(order_id).
  2. Present return details per section F and require explicit confirmation if operation is irreversible/limited.
  3. After confirmation call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids[], payment_method_id).
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user wants to exchange delivered item(s) → THEN:
  1. Present exchange details including replacement new_item_id(s) and price difference; require explicit confirmation for irreversible operations.
  2. After confirmation call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids[], new_item_ids[], payment_method_id).
  3. Observed: exchange_delivered_order_items may be called with the same item_id as new_item_id when exchanging to the same model/variant.

  4. Apply user-specified attribute constraints exactly (do not assume unstated constraints).
  5. IF user asked for "cheapest" / "most affordable" → pick available variant(s) with lowest price among filtered set.
  6. IF user asked for "most expensive" → pick available variant(s) with highest price among filtered set, subject to explicit constraints.

  2. IF the operation is irreversible or one-time-limited (exchange on delivered order, or observed one-time-exchange limits) → explicitly warn and require a clear "yes" from the user before proceeding.

  3. IF user explicitly states a payment preference by exact id (e.g., gift_card_4332117) → use that payment_method_id.

  3. IF user explicitly states a payment preference by exact id (e.g., gift_card_4332117) → use that payment_method_id.

  5. IF user identifies a payment method by position on file (e.g., "use the second card") → ask the user to confirm the last four digits of that card. If user cannot provide last four but explicitly confirms which card in account order to use → you may use the card implied by the user's description. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF user requests a refund to a payment method different from the original method → THEN:
  1. Inform the user refunds are credited to the original payment method by default (and/or to an existing gift card on file). Use wording like: "I'm sorry but this is not possible. Per our policy refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card (if available)."
  2. Offer alternatives: refund to original payment method, refund to gift card on file if present.
  3. IF the user insists on a different refund destination → offer and, if requested, perform escalation: call transfer_to_human_agents with a concise summary (see section P). After transfer, report transfer success if tool returns success. (Observed in Conversation 1)

H. Numeric computations and reporting (required)
- IF totals/differences/sums are needed → call calculate(expression).
- When reporting refunds/charges/exchange_price_difference → present numeric amounts and the payment method that will be charged/credited.
- After return_delivered_order_items or cancel_pending_order that produce refunds → sum refunds (use calculate if needed) and state total refund amount and refunded-to payment_method_id from tool return.
- After exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items that produce exchange_price_difference:
  - IF exchange_price_difference > 0 → tell user numeric additional charge and which payment_method will be charged.
  - IF exchange_price_difference < 0 → tell user numeric refund amount and which payment_method will receive it.
- Do not promise refund timing.

I. Executing after confirmation and post-call summary (required)
- After explicit user confirmation ("yes") and final reconfirmation:
  1. Call the appropriate tool(s) per sections D/E (use the exact tool names).
  2. IF user reduces scope before execution → reconfirm exact items/new_item_ids and payment_method_id and warnings, then call the tool(s).
- After tool returns → read the tool response and summarize:
  - New order status (returned order_id/status)
  - exchange_items / exchange_new_items / exchange_price_difference / return_items / return_payment_method_id (as present)
  - payment_history changes (list additions/refunds and amounts)
  - Numeric totals (per-item and totals)
  - Then ask: "Anything else I can help you with today?"

J. Cancellations of pending orders (required procedure)
- IF user asks to cancel pending order(s) → THEN:
  1. Ask: "What is the reason for the cancellation?" Offer phrasing or choices (e.g., "no longer needed", "ordered by mistake") if helpful. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
  2. After receiving the reason → call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each confirmed pending order.
  3. After tool returns → report new order status and any refund events recorded in payment_history (use calculate if summing).

K. Returns vs exchanges and per-order limits
- IF user requests both return and exchange on items in the same order → THEN inform user you cannot do both actions for the same item(s) at once; present choices and ask which action to apply for that order.
- IF an order/item already has a return/exchange recorded (order.return_items, order.exchange_items, or order.status indicates "return requested" or "exchange requested") → THEN inform the user that the order/item already has a return/exchange in progress and cannot be acted on again in this session.
- IF delivered order → returning/exchanging a subset of items is allowed; call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items with the subset item_ids.
- IF user requests cancellation of a single item from a pending order AND system or agent behavior indicates single-item cancellation is not supported → THEN:
  - Inform the user that partial cancellation is not possible for that order and that cancelling the entire order is required, and offer to cancel the whole order instead.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents warned that only one exchange initiation is allowed for an order (agents asked the user to choose which exchange now).

L. Address changes (account vs orders)
- IF the user requests an account address change AND wants pending orders updated → THEN:

  4. Confirm to user the new account address and list which pending orders were updated.

- IF attempt is made to change addresses for orders with status != "pending" → tell user those orders cannot be modified for address; only pending orders are changed.

M. Multiple orders and sequential handling (required)
- IF the user asks to change multiple orders/items → THEN handle each order sequentially:
  1. Identify matching items per order (use get_order_details).
  2. Present and confirm intended changes for each order separately (use section F).
  3. Perform the appropriate tool call for each order separately.
  4. Summarize results per order.
- Combine multiple item exchanges/modifications for the same order into one exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items call when possible.
- When returning items across different delivered orders → call return_delivered_order_items separately per order.

N. Insufficient payment / balance errors (required)
- IF a modify/exchange/return tool call fails with "insufficient balance" or similar → THEN:
  1. Inform user of failure and present numeric shortfall if provided by tool.
  2. Ask whether user wants to:
     - Provide an alternate payment method and retry, OR
     - Use another payment method on file (present options), OR
     - Be transferred to a human agent.
  3. IF user has no other payment method and asks for help → offer transfer_to_human_agents.
- Observed Behavior: Agents offered transfer to a human when gift card balance insufficient. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

O. Confirmation before irreversible or limited operations (required)
- IF operation is irreversible or one-time-limited (exchange on delivered order, modify pending order when single-modification allowed) → THEN:
  1. Inform the user of irreversibility or single-modification limit.
  2. Require explicit affirmative confirmation ("yes") before calling return_delivered_order_items, exchange_delivered_order_items, or modify_pending_order_items.
  3. IF user confirms → proceed; otherwise cancel action.

P. Tool error handling and transfers (required)
- IF required tool returns "Tool not found" → retry with alternate equivalent observed lookup tool if available (e.g., attempt get_product_details when et_product_details fails). (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF tool returns an error (e.g., insufficient balance) → read and relay the error message and present actionable options (alternate payment, retry, transfer to human).
- IF user explicitly requests escalation or agent cannot complete action (e.g., user demands refund to a different payment method and system disallows) → call:

  - After transfer, inform user transfer succeeded or report any transfer tool response. (Observed Behavior — supported; used in Conversation 1)

Q. Selection and presentation conventions (required)
- Always present numeric results explicitly (per-item prices, price differences, refund amounts, totals, number of available variants).
- When presenting matching orders/items, name order_id and item_id(s) and item status.
- When presenting replacement variants, include item_id, key option fields (attributes), availability, and price.
- After successful tool calls, summarize returned order state exactly as returned and ask "Anything else I can help you with today?"

R. Tracking numbers, cancelled orders, and product attributes (observed & required)
- IF user asks for a tracking number and has no order number → search get_user_details(user_id).orders, call get_order_details for each, and return the tracking_id(s) and their order_id(s) for delivered or cancelled orders (fulfillments may still include tracking_id). (Observed in Conversation 4)
- IF user asks for a product attribute for an item they ordered → read get_order_details(order_id).items[].options and reply with that attribute value.
- IF user asks "how many X options are available" → call list_all_product_types(), then get_product_details(product_id), count variants where available == true, and report the count.

S. Additional concrete behaviors observed (mark where rare)
- IF user requests return of an entire delivered order → collect all item_ids from get_order_details(order_id).items and call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, [all_item_ids], payment_method_id).
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents sometimes stated a one-time-initiation limit for exchanges on orders and warned that after exchange the order cannot be modified further.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents sometimes retried a second email or alternate zip if first lookup failed.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents sometimes requested cancellation reasons as selectable choices ("no longer needed", "ordered by mistake", "financial issues").
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents sometimes selected a payment method on file that matches a user-stated brand without listing all options first.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents sometimes ask whether the user has sufficient balance on a gift card before using it for the payment.
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Exchanges can be initiated where new_item_id == old item_id (exchange to same model) — tool call example exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[id], new_item_ids=[same id], payment_method_id).
- Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory): Agents warned that only one exchange initiation is allowed and asked users to choose which exchange to perform now.

T. Do not assume or add policy beyond the above rules.

End of policy.

Updated operational policy for agent actions (complete, updated)

Principles (apply always)
- Use only behaviors supported by the provided trajectories. Do not add rules not evidenced.
- Every IF → THEN mapping must be concrete: conversation state + retrieved data → exact next actions and tool calls.
- Mark behaviors seen only once or rarely as "Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory)."
- Be explicit and concrete: name the tool calls and the exact prompts/parameters to use.
- Only use the observed tools:
  - find_user_id_by_email
  - find_user_id_by_name_zip
  - get_user_details
  - list_all_product_types
  - get_product_details
  - get_order_details
  - modify_pending_order_items
  - modify_pending_order_address
  - cancel_pending_order
  - return_delivered_order_items
  - exchange_delivered_order_items
  - calculate
  - transfer_to_human_agents
  - modify_user_address
- IF a tool returns "Tool not found" → retry with an equivalent observed lookup tool if available (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory).

A. Identification (required before any account/order action)
- IF user provides an email → THEN:
  1. Call find_user_id_by_email(email).
  2. IF that returns a user_id → immediately call get_user_details(user_id).
  3. IF find_user_id_by_email returns "User not found" or error → reply: "I couldn't find that email — please provide another email or provide first_name + last_name + zip." If user supplies another email, retry find_user_id_by_email(second_email) before switching to name+zip. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory; seen in Conversation 2)
- ELSE IF user provides first_name + last_name + zip → THEN:
  1. Call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip).
  2. IF that returns a user_id → immediately call get_user_details(user_id).
  3. IF no user found → Inform: "No account found for that name + zip. Please provide a different zip or your email." If user gives a new zip, retry find_user_id_by_name_zip with the new zip until found or user declines. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- ELSE → Ask exactly: "Please provide your email, or first name + last name and zip code."
- IF user gives only a nickname → Ask: "I need your full legal first and last name to look up the account." (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

B. After identification (immediately after obtaining a user_id)
- IF a user_id is obtained → THEN:
  1. Call get_user_details(user_id).
  2. Read and store get_user_details(user_id).orders, .payment_methods, .address for subsequent decisions and responses.
  3. When mentioning payment methods to the user, present brand/last_four or id fields as available.

  4. Present matching order_id(s) and item_id(s) and item.status and ask user to confirm which order_id/item_id(s) to act on if ambiguous. Use phrasing like: "I found [item] in order #W2378156 as item_id 9408160950 (status: delivered). Do you want to return or exchange that item from #W2378156?"
- IF user asks "Are they all in the same order?" → perform the search above and respond with which items are in which order_id(s) and their status.
- IF user gives an informal description → THEN filter orders by item.name and present filtered results and ask: "Do you mean [order_id] with item_id(s) [...]?"
- IF user says "most recent" and multiple matching orders → THEN tell user which are delivered vs pending and ask which order_id they mean.
- IF order.status == "cancelled" and user asks to return/exchange → THEN inform them cancelled orders/items cannot be returned/exchanged and report any tracking id present in get_order_details(order_id).fulfillments.

D. Allowed actions by order.status (required)

  1. Call get_order_details(order_id).

  4. If multiple changes for the same pending order in the same session → combine into a single modify_pending_order_items call for that order; present combined plan and require confirmation. (Observed Behavior — supported)
  5. Note: modify_pending_order_items may produce refunds/additional charges (check returned payment_history).
- IF order.status == "pending" AND user wants to change shipping address for that order → THEN present plan and require confirmation, then call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user wants to return delivered item(s) → THEN:
  1. Call get_order_details(order_id).
  2. Present return details per section F and require explicit confirmation for irreversible/limited operations.
  3. After confirmation call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids[], payment_method_id).

  2. After confirmation call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids[], new_item_ids[], payment_method_id).
  3. Observed: exchange_delivered_order_items may be called with the same item_id as new_item_id when exchanging to the same model/variant.
